codeReview.summary: This is a re-run review. The prior review identified 2 blocking bugs: (1) _update_streak() doesn't persist streak freezes/breaks, and (2) _generate_daily_tasks() creates duplicate weak topic tasks. The commit 6673bca only fixed a test file (title field requirement). The service.py code with the blocking bugs remains unchanged - both issues are STILL PRESENT and NOT FIXED.

issues.description: REMAINING UNFIXED: _update_streak() modifies record.streak_freezes_available (line 443), record.streak_freezes_used (line 444), and record.current_streak (line 447) in memory but NEVER calls update_streak_record() to persist these changes. Compare to _update_streak_on_completion() at line 491 which properly calls await self.repo.update_streak_record(). When a streak freeze is used, the decrement of streak_freezes_available and increment of streak_freezes_used are lost. When a streak breaks (current_streak = 0), this is not persisted. The function only returns a dict (line 455) without any database update.

REMAINING UNFIXED: In _generate_daily_tasks(), weak topics are iterated TWICE: First at lines 152-157 for revision tasks (weak_topics[:2]), then at lines 163-167 for lesson tasks (weak_topics[:remaining_slot]). After the first loop adds 2 revision tasks, remaining_slot = 4 - 2 = 2, so the second loop iterates weak_topics[:2] again - the SAME topics. Example: if weak_topics = [A, B, C], revision creates tasks for A and B, then lesson creates tasks for A and B again (since remaining_slot=2 and [:2] gives A, B). The same topic gets BOTH revision AND lesson tasks on the same day, creating duplicates.

summary: RE-RUN REVIEW: Both blocking bugs from the prior review remain UNFIXED. (1) _update_streak() still doesn't persist streak freezes used or streak breaks to the database - changes remain in memory only. (2) _generate_daily_tasks() still creates duplicate tasks when weak_topics appear in both the revision loop and the lesson loop. The commit 6673bca only changed the test file to fix a title field issue, but did not address the service.py bugs.
